candidate_contract.mode_23_read: Full selected-window BF16 P/dP page plus D-parallel contribution consumer, checksum sink, no valid dKV atomics.
candidate_contract.mode_24_read: Same as mode 23, but with valid compressed dKV atomics.
candidate_contract.decision: Do not run mode 24 or promote this branch: mode 23 already exceeds the full sparse-attention backward gate before final dKV atomics.

interpretation.what_was_tested: Whether the retained query-major source boundary can avoid q/grad_output replay by staging the whole selected-window P/dP page once and letting multiple D16 consumer wave groups reuse it.
interpretation.what_failed: The producer page plus D-parallel consumer synchronization is slower than the retained Triton source-boundary schedule even without valid compressed dKV atomics.
interpretation.why_it_matters: This rejects a tempting shared-scorepage workaround. The next useful kernel needs to stay closer to the Triton H64/N64/D128 query-major inner schedule and attach a lighter group-aware epilogue, not introduce a full selected-window page inside the CTA.

streaming_boundary_note.read: A final sync between split dQ and dKV branches is not mathematically required. The next consumers can wait on the specific event for the tensor they read. A sync is only introduced by framework/autograd integration if the custom op wrapper exposes both outputs as one completed boundary or if a downstream fused consumer requires both tensors at launch.
